verdict_reasoning: STIM1 is a single-pass TM ER-calcium sensor that translocates to the plasma membrane upon ER calcium depletion, a canonical store-operated calcium entry (SOCE) activation trigger. During this activated state, STIM1 accumulates at ER-PM junctions and persists at the PM surface via its own TM domain, with its ectodomain exposed on the outer leaflet. Surface biotinylation and live-cell imaging confirm PM residence during activation. Baseline state is ER-resident; PM pool is activation-induced and reversible.
